100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 15, 1926, political tensions escalated in Britain as the General Strike continued, disrupting transportation and essential services nationwide. In the United States, President Coolidge faced mounting criticism over his administration's handling of labor issues amid rising strikes. Meanwhile, in Germany, the Weimar Republic grappled with economic instability, as hyperinflation threatened the livelihoods of many citizens. These events reflect the tumultuous social and political climate of the era.
